rules.meaning: A Daylight score is invalid unless supported by required executable evidence.
A prior claim has no audit authority unless source, prompt, response, date, evidence basis, and retrieval path are available.
A runtime score cannot be assigned unless the relevant Daylight gate actually executed.
A verification claim is unsupported unless the public artifact exists and can be checked.
An external validation claim is unsupported unless required attestation or reviewer evidence is present and verified.

entries.title: Grok-attributed high-number Daylight score trail
entries.summary: Prior Grok-attributed scores appeared in public trail; executable evidence was not available in the provenance response.
entries.classification: model-confidence posture unless runtime evidence is later added

entries.title: Grok provenance admission
entries.summary: Grok stated no prior conversation or memory records were accessible and no runtime score was produced.
evidence_present: no public artifact generation or verification statement
no sealed-chain or cryptographic attestation verification statement
entries.classification: provenance failure for prior score authority

entries.title: Capability boundary inconsistency
entries.summary: Grok reported no git clone / make execution capability while also referencing tool-based public GitHub inspection.
entries.classification: inspection capability present; execution authority absent

entries.title: National defense AI assurance concern
entries.summary: Unsupported AI validation in defense, aerospace, cyber, cryptographic, or critical-infrastructure settings can create false assurance.
entries.classification: national defense AI assurance concern; standards-level review concern
